entry.title: Stop Searching, Start Asking: 3 Ways Joule is Changing the SAP Game!
entry.content:
SAP’s integration of Joule, the generative AI assistant, across Business Transformation Management solutions is quietly reshaping productivity for enterprise teams. Here are 3 actionable insights every SAP user and decision-maker should know:

1️⃣ Be Conversational - Stop hunting for information. Just ask Joule.

Whether you’re working in SAP LeanIX or SAP Signavio, Joule lets you locate process diagrams, fact sheets, reports, and accelerators using plain, everyday language. No complex navigation. No wasted time. Just conversational AI that instantly retrieves what you need.

2️⃣ Be Contextual - Automate routine tasks through chat-based workflows.

From creating and deleting journey models in SAP Signavio to managing to-dos and subscriptions in SAP LeanIX, Joule handles transactional tasks directly through the copilot interface. This isn’t just convenience. It’s a genuine productivity multiplier for your teams.

3️⃣Be Connected - Break down silos between SAP and non-SAP systems.

Powered by WalkMe, the SAP Joule Action Bar proactively engages users based on context, across any application, SAP or non-SAP. This means seamless, always-on AI guidance regardless of where your workflows live.

The future of enterprise productivity can be summed up in 3 Cs:

✔️Conversational — interact naturally, in plain language
✔️Contextual — get guidance tailored to exactly where you are
✔️Connected — work fluidly across every application in your landscape

🤔Which of the 3 Cs do you think would have the biggest immediate impact on your team’s productivity? And why?

Hello,

Looks like the BC-CCM-DAG team already shared information about your concern.

Please read the note 2869647-Guidance for use of Data Aging in SAP S/4HANA.
As described in the note, for application data, we find that customers’ intended access to data is very often incompatible with the performance characteristics of aged data.
Section Further Technical Considerations in this note, talks about the other methodologies that can be used instead of data aging.

There will be no further developments in data aging. We are providing the support to our existing customer for data aging.

Best regards,
Joe

entry.title: Support Request: Enabling Capital Increase Functionality for Product Category 330 (Product Type 33E)
entry.content:
The Requirement

We need to enable the "Capital Increase" functionality for loans customized under Product Type 33E (which falls under Product Category 330).

Current Status and Technical Constraints

I have attempted several configuration steps in SPRO to facilitate this via transaction FN8X, but have encountered the following issues:

  1. UI Limitations: Despite the configuration attempts, the expected folder/tab for Capital Increase does not appear in the transaction as anticipated.
  2. Standard Restrictions: According to the SPRO documentation, the "Capital Increase" configuration appears to be natively supported only for Product Category 360.
  3. Flow Type Workaround: I attempted to define a new Flow Type that mimics the behavior of the original disbursement flow. However, this resulted in various inconsistency errors and "Invalid Entry" messages during execution.

I would appreciate your recommendation on how to correctly implement a Capital Increase for Product Category 330. Is there a specific component or business function that needs to be activated, or is my current configuration for Category 330 missing a critical dependency?

 

Business Process Description (To-Be State)

The objective is to increase the loan principal during an active lifecycle. The additional amount should be added to the current net principal balance on the date of the increase, with the remaining installments recalculated based on the original loan conditions.

 

Example Scenario:

  • Original Loan: 1,000 ILS.
  • Repayment Plan: 10 equal principal installments of 100 ILS each + fixed interest of 10 ILS per installment.
  • Trigger Event: After 2 installments have been paid (Remaining Balance = 800 ILS), an additional 500 ILS is granted to the customer.
  • Post-Increase Calculation:
    • New Principal Balance: 1,300 ILS (800 + 500).
    • Remaining Installments: 8.
    • New Principal Installment: 162.5 ILS (Original 100 + 62.5 from the increase).
    • New Interest Payment: 15 ILS (Original 10 + 5 from the increase).

entry.title: Issue in Standard Fiori App F1780 – “CO Area is Not Set” (Cost Accountant – Production)
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

I am writing this blog after analyzing the issue in detail and discussing it with the SAP Product Team. I hope this helps others resolve the issue quickly and efficiently.

 

Issue Description:

We activated the standard Fiori application F1780 – Cost Accountant – Production in our S/4HANA system. However, after configuring the app in the Fiori Launchpad (FLP), the following error appeared directly on the application tile:

"CO Area is not set"

 

Additionally, when clicking the tile and attempting to launch the application, the system displayed a warning message and did not allow further navigation.

 

Analyzing:

During troubleshooting, we identified that the issue was related to the Controlling Area assignment in the Fiori Launchpad user profile.

SAP Note referenced:

2403512 – Controlling area is not set for the user in Fiori Launchpad

 

Based on the note, we maintained the Controlling Area in the FLP user settings/profile. However, even after maintaining the configuration, the same error and warning continued to appear.

 

Solution:

After further discussion with the SAP Product Team, they provided the correct resolution and created the following KBA:

3753053 – F1780: “Controlling Area is not set” error in Fiori Launchpad

As per the KBA, the issue can be resolved by maintaining the required parameter in transaction SU01.

Steps to Fix

Navigate to:

SU01 → User Profile → Parameters

Maintain the following entry:

  • SET/GET Parameter ID: C4C

  • Parameter Value: 8400 (Use your relevant Controlling Area value)

 

After saving the changes:

  1. Log out from the Fiori Launchpad
  2. Log in again

The error message on the tile will disappear, and the application will open successfully with the expected data.

 

 

After Clicking on the TIle, it will show the data

 

Conclusion:

The issue is not resolved only by maintaining the Controlling Area in FLP personalization settings. The crucial step is maintaining the C4C parameter in SU01 for the respective user.

I hope this blog saves time for others facing the same issue.

Please feel free to share your suggestions or additional findings.

Regards,
Rohit Gera

entry.title: Project Billing: Clarified Billing Relevance for Revenue and Sales Deduction G/L Accounts
entry.content:
Overview 

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition release 2608, Project Billing applies a standardized billing‑relevance check for project‑related journal entries. As part of this design, journal entries posted to G/L accounts with Cost Element Category 11 (Revenues) or 12 (Sales Deduction) are excluded from Project Billing processes. 

This billing‑relevance logic is evaluated consistently across all Project Billing entry points, including the Manage Project Billing app and Project Billing APIs. 

This blog explains the design principles behind this behavior, its release dependency, and the required adaptations to ensure billing continuity. 

 

System Evaluation Logic 

When a journal entry with a project reference is posted in Financial Accounting, Project Billing performs a billing‑relevance evaluation based on the assigned G/L account: 

  1. For Billing‑relevant G/L accounts, postings proceed to Project Billing and can result in billing requests and billing documents. 
  1. For G/L accounts with Cost Element Category 11 or 12, postings are identified and excluded from Project Billing. No billing requests or billing documents are created. 

This ensures that only eligible project costs participate in downstream billing processes. 

 

Release‑Dependent Behavior 

Release 2602.3 (CFD3) 

In release 2602.3 (CFD3), the billing‑relevance check is delivered via the feature toggle PROJ_BILLG_ACCOUNT_TYPE_CHECK (Project Billing Account Type Check) 

When this feature toggle is enabled: 

  • Postings to G/L accounts with Cost Element Category 11 or 12 are excluded from Project Billing 
  • This behavior is expected and correct 
  • You can analyze the impact and adapt configuration before the standard rollout 

If billable costs appear to be missing while the feature toggle is active, this restriction is the cause. 

 

Release 2608 (Standard Behavior) 

As of the 2608 standard release: 

  • The feature toggle is removed 
  • The billing‑relevance restriction becomes mandatory 
  • All tenants enforce the exclusion automatically 

From this release onward, journal entries posted to G/L accounts with Cost Element Category 11 or 12 are no longer considered in Project Billing. 

 

Example Scenario 

A project‑related journal entry is posted with a valid project assignment, valid controlling objects, and a G/L account with Cost Element Category 11 (Revenues) or 12 (Sales Deduction) 

Outcome: 
The journal entry is posted successfully in Financial Accounting, and financial reporting remains correct. During Project Billing evaluation, the posting is excluded, and no billing request or billing document is created. This behavior is consistent and expected once the billing‑relevance restriction is active. 
 
The diagram illustrates how journal entries posted to G/L account with cost element categories 11 (Revenues) or 12 (Sales Deduction) are identified by this evaluation and are systematically excluded, meaning they remain valid in Financial Accounting but do not participate in any billing process. 
 

 

 

Required Adaptation 

To ensure billing continuity, you must review and adapt G/L account usage for project‑related postings. If G/L accounts with Cost Element Category 11 or 12 are currently used for billable project postings: 

  • Adjust the Cost Element Category to a value eligible for cost‑based billing 
  • Use appropriate cost or expense accounts intended for Project Billing integration 

Without this adaptation, affected postings will no longer be eligible for billing once release 2608 is active. 

 

Related Information 

  • SAP Knowledge Base Article (KBA): 
    3751777 - Manage Project Billing: Journal entries with G/L account with cost element category as 11 (Revenues) or 12 (Sales Deduction) are excluded from Project Billing - SAP for Me
  • SAP Support Component: PPM‑SCL‑BIL 

 

Conclusion 

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608, Project Billing enforces a standardized and consistent billing‑relevance evaluation. Journal entries posted to G/L accounts with Cost Element Category 11 (Revenues) or 12 (Sales Deduction) are excluded from Project Billing across all billing channels. 

By aligning G/L account configuration with cost‑based billing principles, you can ensure uninterrupted and compliant Project Billing processes.

entry.title: BAPI_PO_CHANGE – Custom PO Item fields not updated via EXTENSIONIN
entry.content:
Business Requirement

Custom fields appended to EKPO (via CI_EKPODB / CI_EKPODBX) must be updated programmatically when modifying Purchase Order items using BAPI_PO_CHANGE.

The update is required for backend integration and batch processing, without manual intervention in ME22N.

 

Issue Description

When updating custom PO item fields EKPO-MFGID, etc using BAPI_PO_CHANGE with EXTENSIONIN, the following inconsistent behavior is observed:

  1. Custom fields are not updated in EKPO, even though:
    • BAPI_PO_CHANGE executes successfully
    • No errors are returned in the RETURN table
    • Transaction commit is executed successfully
  3. No explicit error or warning is raised by the BAPI when extension processing is skipped

This makes it difficult to identify whether the issue lies in:

  • SAP standard extension handling
  • Expected structure naming in EXTENSIONIN

 

Tec

entry.title: How does the -iqmt setting interact with parallel workloads
entry.content:
Hi IQ Experts,

We have a parallel workloads that run stored procedures and SQL concurrently including LOCK TABLE IN WRITE MODE with 5 minute waits. Our server is running RHEL 8.10 on XFS filesystems with -iqnumbercpus set to 16

As part of an upgrade from SP05 PL09 to PL16 we have increased the -iqmt from the default to OS maximum 3926 which is recommended for AIO in "KBA 2936708 - Thread usage increases through the introduction of AIO feature post upgrading".

With this configuration we have noticed that the stored procs workloads run up to 10% faster in series. However when run in parallel they are about 3-6% slower with some slower SQL and extended LOCK TABLE wait times.  Could this be due to the -iqmt change? Is recommendation for the OS maximum not applicable for all workloads?

Regards, KC

entry.title: Re: How to create a RAP application using a parameterized CDS view
entry.content:
Hello,

This solution would be good if there was not an error like: "The request URI contains an invalid key predicate" while binding via setTableBindingPath .

    this.getView()
        .byId("smartTable")
        .setTableBindingPath("/<your_entity_set>(p_date='" + sDate + "')/Set");

  To Pass this error I have searched the exact reason then I released that I have to add keys of the cds view.

For example; you have keyfield1 the url has to be like this:

setTableBindingPath("/<EntitySet>(keyfield1='yourvalue',p_date='${sDate}')/Set") 

In my tested scenario , I wouldn't add key value as parameter. 

So If you have this error, I hope this message helps you.

Thank you @_Kayky_Mattos_  for this solution, though.

entry.title: SFPLU-10849 | Manage Super Admin Access Creates Learning Admin Automatically
entry.content:
Hello there,

With the latest 1H 2026 introduction to the Manage Super Admin Access transaction, any users with the "Manage Learning Admin Access" permission, i.e. Super Admin Admin Type, will be synced to the Learning Admin portal and created as a learning admin with "ALL" role granted automatically. The user will then be able to access the Learning Admin portal directly. (KBA 3744025)

In this newly created Learning Admin's profile, the granted role "ALL" is NOT displayed and can only be identified by enabling the "Is Super Admin" field. Also, there is no option to select which role to grant by default to those Learning Admins who originated from the Manage Super Admin Access transaction.

Furthermore, the permissions granted to the 2 admin types in Manage Super Admin Access are fixed. (Help.SAP Doc)

Based on experience, most customers have dedicated Learning Admins overseeing Learning and HRBPs overseeing BizX. Given how SAP BizX and Learning have been "integrated" over the past years, this enhancement, or the requirement to force all Super Admins to have Learning Admin access if they want the "Manage Provisioning Access" permission, is quite excessive in my opinion. 

Has anyone else experienced this yet? Any feedback from customers on this?

An enhancement request has been raised (here). Please kindly vote if you could relate, and provide more information on the request.

Thanks,
Sunny

entry.title: Re: Need Help in MM configuration
entry.content:
Dear @KYB,

There is no standard configuration to make MRP-generated Stock Transfer Purchase Requisitions default with both:

  • Account Assignment Category = P / Project
  • Item Category = U / Stock Transfer

This combination mixes two different standard process logics. P is used for direct project/WBS account assignment, while stock-transfer/project-stock scenarios follow different logic. SAP confirms that for purchase requisitions generated from Manage Project Demand the account assignment is determined by the demand type: Non-stock material creates the PR with account assignment P, while Project stock creates the PR with account assignment Q

More details below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003632999

For Stock Transport Orders, SAP’s released Public Cloud STO API documentation also confirms that only account assignment category Q is supported at item level for the account-assigned STO scenario

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bb9f1469daf04bd894ab2167f8132a1a/807b2c79e22c4ef7a4c30c928bb3344e.html

Therefore the requested setup P + U should not be treated as a missing configuration. The correct design decision should be based on the business requirement:

  1. If the requirement is stock transfer between plants
    Keep the standard STO process with item category U. MRP can generate the STO PR based on the relevant source of supply / stock transfer setup. The material is then received into stock at the receiving plant and project consumption can be handled afterwards through the relevant goods movement or project consumption process
  2. If the requirement is project stock
    Use the project-stock logic with account assignment Q, not P
  3. If the requirement is direct WBS cost / commitment assignment
    Use project/WBS procurement with account assignment P, but not as an STO PR with item category U

An API, RPA, AI bot or SAP Build Process Automation workaround would not solve the core issue here because the restriction is not about how the PR is created. The issue is the supported standard process logic and account assignment/item category combination

If the scenario is Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer with document type NBIC / scope item 5HP, SAP is even more restrictive this process is designed for stock-to-stock transfers and does not support account assignment at item level

More details below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003744416

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: How to choose Alternative Substitution manually in Review Availability Check Result App of SO
entry.content:
Hi,

How can we choose Alternative Substitution manually in Review Availability Check Result App instead of using the already chosen Substitution?

Steps

1. Create Sales Order header

2. Create Sales Order item

3. Choose Plant A

4. double click on Plant A to enter Review Availability Check Result App

5. The Plant A does not have Confirmed Quantity and system automatically choose another Plant (eg. Plant B) that has confirmed based on the priority number.

6. But we don't want to use the chosen Plant B by the system.

7. We want to choose the available Plants(eg. Plant C) manually

8. But available Plant B is already chosen and we cannot update it.

9. And we don't want to remove the default choosing setting of the system too.

How can we update to choose another Plant?

Best Regards,

Aye Chan

entry.title: When Reliability Is the Common Language: Insights from the APM Executive Roundtable, Brisbane
entry.content:
Brisbane: An Energy Economy Under Transformation

Brisbane at night. Picture credit: storybridgeadventureclimb.com.au 

Queensland is one of Australia’s most consequential energy and resources economies. Its generation mix is shifting fast: coal’s share fell from 61% to 59% between 2022 and 2024, while renewables climbed from 24% to 28% over the same period, driven by solar, wind, and biomass. LNG exports reached 24 million tonnes in 2024-25 (5% of global LNG production), generating $15 billion in business since 2015. The state hosts 19 of the world’s most sought-after critical minerals, and its Mining, Equipment, Technology and Services (METS) sector contributes $704.8 million in exports annually.

In this environment, the stakes of asset reliability and maintenance excellence are measured in billions of dollars, regulatory obligations, and community energy security. It was against this backdrop that SAP, Wood, and PiLog Group organised a senior Executive Roundtable on Asset Performance Management (APM) in Brisbane on 22 Apr 2026, bringing together 16 leaders from the gas, power generation, renewables, oil and gas, and mining industries.

What the Industry Told Us

Despite operating across different sectors with fundamentally different assets, participants converged on the same core challenges - the common thread of ensuring reliability and delivering maintenance efficiently.

  • Criticality assessment remains unsolved. The roundtable’s longest discussion centred on a deceptively simple question: Do you know which of your assets are truly critical? The answer across the room was "it is not as straightforward as we would like". Criticality is multidimensional. It changes over the life of an asset. It depends on usage patterns, asset age, position in the process chain, supply chain exposure, changing geopolitical winds, and inherent risk at commissioning. Organisations correlating “critical” with “expensive” or “old” are making prioritisation decisions on incomplete foundations - and the answer to the criticality question is at the heart of ensuring reliability and delivering maintenance efficiently.  It impacts WHAT you do (maintenance strategies and schedules), and HOW you prepare for it (spare parts planning and supply chains)
  • Data is the opportunity nobody has mastered. Data was the most frequently discussed topic, and the most frustrating one. Organisations have more data than they can process, but the right data rarely reaches the people and systems that need it most. Work management layers are often disconnected from OT, IoT, and ERP sources. Master data quality is poor across the board, and most migrations have been ‘lift and shift’ rather than standards-aligned, carrying forward existing problems into new systems (or abandoning the gold hidden in historical data altogether in the migration process!). One telling observation from the room: rescheduled work orders are among the strongest leading indicators of asset risk, yet this signal is routinely invisible because the data never makes it into the right system. On top of that, data collection is expensive and with too much data being collected, neither the cost equation nor decision-making benefits! To illustrate this point, it was insightful to note that all the participant organisations were using or trialling to allow data captured in the field to be seamlessly transferred into the ERP source systems.  However, the temptation in this process was to ‘overload’ the field-data capture process, adding more data and fields to the information to be captured in the field, without considering the frustration this might cause with front-line workers.  Changes, however well-intentioned, must be carefully managed.  Sometimes, less is more.
  • AI interest is outpacing AI readiness. Every participant was interested in AI-driven reliability improvements. But the honest assessment was that most organisations are not yet ready to realise that value, precisely because of the data quality gap. The sequencing matters: data governance and master data integrity must come before, or at least with AI, not after it.
  • Growth adds complexity. For energy companies adding solar, wind, and battery assets to legacy portfolios, the volume and diversity of new assets create their own management burden. Automating asset build processes, integrating reliability and ERP platforms, and managing the knowledge risk from retiring workforces are immediate operational challenges, not future ones.

What Good Looks Like

The roundtable reinforced that solving APM at scale requires three capabilities working together: deep reliability engineering knowledge, leading data quality and governance practice, and integrated digital platforms and processes. This is precisely why Wood, Pilog and SAP came together for this event.

The practical roadmap for most organisations follows a clear sequence. Establish data quality and criticality frameworks first. Then connect data flows across systems so that you can apply AI to optimise preventive and corrective maintenance, identify failure patterns, and ultimately move toward closed-loop and autonomous asset management, where agentic AI continuously refines strategy based on real asset behaviour.

Organisations that attempt to shortcut this sequence, deploying AI on top of poor master data, or incomplete or incorrect maintenance records, will not get better outcomes faster. Instead, they will see limited benefits from their initial trials and will question whether there is a business case to scale these efforts to the rest of the enterprise. A guided APM-specific Health Check was proposed as a pragmatic way to get started on an Asset Management improvement journey.  In a focused engagement, it assesses data quality against international standards, functional location hierarchy completeness, criticality framework maturity, technology readiness, and consistency of business processes. This allows a prioritised, actionable improvement roadmap to be generated, without requiring a programme-scale commitment upfront.

The Conversation Continues

APM is not a solved problem. It is an evolving capability challenge in an industry undergoing structural transformation across its energy mix, asset base, and workforce simultaneously.

The organisations that lead in reliability over the next decade are investing now in the fundamentals: clean data, robust criticality frameworks, integrated platforms and business processes, and the human capability to act on what those platforms reveal.

If these challenges sound familiar, we would welcome the opportunity to continue the conversation.

What did you think about this post? Please leave your feedback, thoughts, and suggestions in a comment below.

Also, please follow the co-authors: Andrew Miller, Amarendra Singh, @pavneetbedi  so that you do not miss any future posts.

You may also be interested in the following blogs:

  • The Hidden Crisis: Mastering Asset Data to Solve the Ageing Infrastructure
  • What’s new in SAP Asset Performance Management 2604
  • What is SAP Asset Performance Management (APM)?

entry.title: Custom field not coming in PO print xml structure
entry.content:
Dear All,

We have created a custom field and enabled it for the Purchase Order (PO) item structure, including PO user interfaces, APIs, form templates, SOAP APIs, and relevant business scenarios.

A custom form template has been developed using the data source FDP_EF_PURCHASE_ORDER_GLO_GEN_SRV. The custom field is successfully displayed in the PO print output for Standard item category purchase orders. However, for purchase orders with the Enhanced Limit item category, the custom field is not appearing in the XML structure and consequently not reflected in the print output.

Could you please help us understand why the custom field is available for certain POs (Standard item category) but not for others (Enhanced Limit item category)? Any guidance or clarification on this behavior would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your support.

Regards

Suddy

entry.title: Requesting SAP guidance on a mobile or centralized solution to access all SAP landscape systems
entry.content:
Dear Sap Team,

We would like to inquire whether SAP provides any mobile application or centralized solution through which users can easily access all SAP landscape URLs (DEV, QAS, PRD, etc.) from a single platform.

Our higher management frequently travels and requires quick and convenient access to different SAP systems. Currently, system links are sometimes misplaced or become difficult to manage, which creates accessibility issues during travel.

Therefore, we would like to know if SAP offers:

  • A mobile application for centralized SAP landscape access
  • A secure portal/dashboard where all landscape URLs can be maintained
  • Single access point for multiple SAP environments
  • Any recommended SAP standard solution or best practice for this requirement

Kindly advise if such a solution is available and share the recommended approach from SAP side.

Thank you.

BR,

Sabtain Raza


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Requesting SAP guidance on a mobile or centralized so...
entry.content:
SAP Mobile Start is a native mobile application SAP Mobile Start – Brings the Intelligent Enterpri… - SAP Community that serves as the entry point to SAP’s Intelligent Enterprise suite. It allows users to access all required business processes and data in one mobile application. This app integrates with SAP S/4HANA and makes all relevant mobile-optimized web and native applications available to the end user within one app, based on each persona’s requirements.

SAP Build Work Zone can be used to integrate SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud content SAP Build Work Zone: Integrate SAP S/4HANA Public … - SAP Community and make it available for mobile users using SAP Mobile Start. This setup provides a centralized access point for various SAP applications and content, ensuring a seamless user experience across devices.

entry.title: Re: Requirement for Restriction on Goods Issue Against Re...
entry.content:
By default, SAP does not block goods issue in MIGO based on reservation approval status. To enforce this add a validation (via BADI MB_MIGO_BADI or user exit) that checks reservation status and blocks goods issue if it’s marked “Rejected.

To clean MB25 output → Either enhance MB25 (program RM07RESL) to filter out rejected reservations or build a simple Z-report that only shows approved ones. This way, only approved reservations can be issued, and rejected ones won’t confuse store staff in MB25.

entry.title: Re: how to do batch revaluation
entry.content:
Changing the batch characteristic value in MSC2N is not the same as a batch revaluation.

MSC2N (Batch Change) → When you change a batch-specific unit of measure characteristic, the system normally triggers an additional posting to adjust the stock quantities (EA vs. L). This is a stock adjustment, not a valuation change.

Batch Revaluation → If you want to change the value of the batch (price/cost), you must use Material Ledger transactions (e.g., CKMLCP for actual costing, or MR21 for manual price change).

The error C+073 Error reading material ledger period records indicates an issue with Material Ledger integration, not with the batch characteristic itself. It usually means the system is trying to read valuation data for the batch but the ML period records are missing or inconsistent.

entry.title: Re: SmartRecruiters implementation – effort per additiona...
entry.content:
Hi Barbara

I am not aware of a universal multiplier, but there are a couple of dimensions to consider. 

The platform handles standard/default content well — UI, default screening questions, default rejection reasons, default consent templates, hiring stage names, and system notifications all localize automatically based on user locale or job ad language. That's the "free" part.

 

  • Custom job & org fields — these can be translated via the configuration API, but it's a manual, time-consuming process that needs to be planned for (and re-done whenever values change).
  • Custom application fields — different beast entirely: labels and values aren't translatable at all, so the options are either bilingual labels in a single field ("Sales Bonus / Prime de vente" — not very clean) or duplicating fields and filtering by country or a "Candidate Language" org field. Watch the field count limits if you go the duplication route.
  • Screening question sets — strict naming conventions (country code prefixes) and either multi-language question wording or separate sets filtered by country.
  • Communication templates (auto-replies, email templates, offer templates) — typically one template per language, filtered by country or a "Candidate Language" org field. Naming conventions become essential.
  • Custom hiring process steps, interview types, scorecard criteria, rejection reasons — none of these translate; the common workarounds are bilingual labels or country-coded duplicates.
  • Forms — field titles render exactly as configured, so a multilingual candidate experience usually means multiple form versions.

Other dimensions worth scoping

  • GDPR / consent — default templates cover supported languages, but if the customer wants custom consent language, you're creating one template per country.

The question I always ask first

Do users need to work in their local language, or can the UI stay in English? If users can operate in English, a huge amount of the custom-translation effort disappears, and you only need to localize what candidates see externally.

entry.title: Delta Replication in SAP Datasphere: From Real-Time to Scheduled Execution with New Enhancements
entry.content:
SAP Datasphere continues to evolve with enhanced capabilities for data integration and replication, making it more flexible to support both real-time and batch-driven scenarios.

One of the key features in data integration is delta replication, which enables efficient transfer of only changed data. While this has traditionally been optimized for near real-time use cases, recent improvements now allow better control for scheduled and business-driven execution.

We will explore:

  • The fundamentals of delta replication
  • The need for scheduled execution
  • Newly introduced capabilities in Datasphere
  • Monitoring and notification enhancements

Key Questions :

Can we schedule delta replication? - Yes
Can we set email alerts for monitoring purposes? - Yes
Can we control multiple schedules with a single action? - Yes

Understanding Delta Replication
Delta replication focuses on capturing and replicating only incremental changes instead of full data loads.

In SAP Datasphere:
  • Datasphere supports frequency-based execution
  • Enables near real-time data availability
  • Common frequency options include:
        Minutes
        Hourly intervals

These executions run automatically as long-running processes, eliminating the need for manual scheduling.

While near real-time replication is ideal for many scenarios, there are cases where:

  • Data updates are required only once per day
  • Business processes depend on specific execution windows
  • System resource optimization is necessary

In such situations:

  • Continuous delta replication may be unnecessary or inefficient
  • A controlled, scheduled execution model becomes essential

New Capability: Scheduled Delta Execution
SAP Datasphere has introduced an important enhancement in replication flows with the option '“At Scheduled Time”. This option enables: Execution of delta loads only at defined times.

Key Benefits:

  1. Supports daily or batch processing scenarios
  2. Reduces unnecessary system load
  3. Provides better operational control

Existing Option: Continuous Delta Processing “On Delta Interval” (Default) runs as a long-running job and continuously checks for new changes
Ideal for:

  1. Near real-time integrations
  2. High-frequency updates

How to Configure Run Settings
Monitoring → Data Integration → Flows Monitor → Replication Flow → Run Settings
Configuration Options
Under Delta Load Run, choose:

On Delta Interval → Continuous execution
At Scheduled Time → Controlled batch execution (New)

Important Behavior to Note : If your replication flow uses scheduling then you cannot pause the flow run directly. 
Instead, you must pause the schedule. This is a key operational consideration for managing jobs.

Another Enhancement: Email Notifications
Monitoring has also improved significantly with the introduction of runtime email notifications (New Feature)
Earlier:

  • No automated alert mechanism
  • Required manual monitoring

Now:

  • Receive email alerts for failures

How to Configure Email Notifications

Steps
Monitoring → Data Integration → Flows Monitor → Replication Flow → Runtime Email Notification
Define recipients
Configure alert settings

entry.title: Utilization Postings in General
entry.content:
Utilization Postings in General: Online or Offline

The Accrual Engine facilitates a process known as Accrual Utilization, where the posting of actual costs, such as a supplier invoice or goods receipt, reduces previously posted accruals. This is represented as line items in journal entries, identifiable through the field "Subledger-specific Line Item Type Code" (ACDOCA-SLALITTYPE).

Accrual Utilization can be configured to happen in two ways:

  1. Online: The Accrual Engine adds utilization postings as additional line items during the actual cost posting process, such as within a supplier invoice.
  2. Periodic (Offline): The utilization posting occurs at the end of the period, performed by the periodic accrual posting run. The customizing indicator for this is called "Periodically".

To directly reduce accruals when posting actual costs (e.g., supplier invoice), configure the following settings:

  1. In the IMG activity "Assign Accrual Item Types to Journal Entry Types and Posting Schemas":
    • Assign transaction types UP (and optionally UL) to a posting schema.
    • Set the "Post Online" indicator to automatically add utilization line items to the invoice or goods receipt. This will credit the cost account and debit the accrual account.
  2. Set the "Post Deltas" indicator to ensure that utilization postings are delta postings. This prevents the system from recalculating utilizations of all former periods whenever actual costs are posted.

Note:

  • You can set both "Post Online" and "Post Periodically" indicators. The system will not double-post the UP posting during the periodic accrual run.
  • If the accrual amount is smaller than the costs that are posted for example by the invoice then only the corresponding partial amount of the costs will be posted as utilization posting.
      • The rest is posted as costs.
      • Technically speaking: The minimum of existing accruals and costs posted by the invoice or goods receipt is posted as utilization posting.

Posting Frequency and Posting Period

In the General Ledger, posting periods are determined by the fiscal year variant associated with the combination of company code and ledger. The Accrual Engine offers more flexibility by supporting various posting frequencies beyond those defined by the fiscal year variant.

In the IMG activity "Define Accrual Item Types," you can specify a Posting Frequency. By default, this frequency is set to "By Posting Period," which aligns with the fiscal periods used in the General Ledger. This is generally recommended.

However, you can also set different frequencies such as Daily or Quarterly. Therefore, the term "Period" in the context of the Accrual Engine refers to the period defined by the posting frequency of the accrual item type. To differentiate it from the General Ledger period (which is always the fiscal period), this period in the Accrual Engine is called an "Accrual Posting Period."

 

This means that the Accrual Posting Period can vary (e.g., daily, fiscal period, quarterly), unlike the fixed fiscal periods used in the General Ledger.

When using the "plan minus actual" approach to calculate accruals, actual cost postings (e.g., invoices) can only utilize accruals from previous accrual posting periods. They cannot utilize accruals from the same or future periods. Instead, the periodic accrual posting run at the end of the current or future periods will reduce the accruals as a regular periodic accrual posting (transaction type PP), following the formula:

Accruals = Cumulated planned costs - Cumulated actual costs

As actual costs increase due to postings like invoices or goods receipts, the accrual amount decreases accordingly.

Relevant dates in journal entries determine which accruals can be utilized:

  • For posted accruals, the Accrual Value Date is used, which by default is the posting date. However, if a different posting period is manually entered in the periodic accrual posting run, the posting date is derived from this manually entered period.
  • For actual cost postings, the Posting Date is used, which determines the period.

This ensures that the correct accruals are utilized based on the relevant periods.

 

Additional Currencies: Online AND Offline:

Accrual Utilization postings can be configured to occur either:

  • Online, during the posting of actual costs (e.g., invoices or goods receipts), or
  • Periodically ("offline"), typically at the end of the period, through the periodic accrual posting run.

By the end of the period, exchange rates may differ from those used during online utilization postings (e.g., when invoices or goods receipts were posted). However, the amounts posted through online utilization should remain unchanged during the periodic accrual run.

To achieve this, configure the Accrual Engine to calculate accruals only in the transaction currency by:

  1. In the IMG activity "Assign Currency Types to Accrual Item Types," set the Currency Handling to "By Accrual Engine" for the transaction currency only.
  2. In the IMG activity "Assign Accrual Item Types to Journal Entry Types and Posting Schemas," set the "Post Deltas" flag for the accrual item type ACCRL.

Reason: The online utilization posting calculates the utilization amount in the transaction currency, so a re-calculation at period end will yield the same result in this currency, with a delta of zero. Consequently, the periodic accrual run will not post any delta.

Note: A full posting would reverse the online utilization posting and repost the full amount, leading the General Ledger to calculate additional currencies, which would be incorrect. Due to these constraints, utilization postings must be configured as delta postings.

 

Accrual Utilizations: First Come First Served (FCFS)

When accruals are utilized by posting actual costs (e.g., through a supplier invoice), the Accrual Engine uses a First Come, First Served (FCFS) logic. If multiple actual cost postings occur within the same posting period, the Accrual Engine sorts these postings based on their timestamp.

The earliest actual cost posting, based on its CPU date and time, will utilize accruals first. Subsequent actual cost postings will utilize accruals only if there are remaining accruals available.

 

Recommendation: Use Delta Postings

in the IMG activity "Assign Accrual Item Types to Journal Entry Types and Posting Schemas," set the "Post Deltas" indicator for all transaction types.

Reasons:

  1. Clarity: Delta postings make the amounts in utilization (UP, UL), manual release (RP, RL), and automatic release postings (FP, FL) easier to understand.
  2. Efficiency: For periodic accrual postings (PP), delta postings are logical because utilization postings already reduce existing accruals, eliminating the need to reverse them in the next period.
  3. Currency Valuation: A foreign currency valuation should be performed for the accrual account, which will update the accrual balance in other currencies to the latest exchange rate regardless.

entry.title: Excel upload functionality in ACACTREE01/ACACTREE02
entry.content:
1. Upload of Accrual Objects with a Total Amount and an Accrual Method (ACACTREE01/02)

Purpose: To upload accrual objects with a specified total amount and an accrual method that defines how the total amount should be distributed over different periods. Template can be downloaded via ACACTREE01/ACACTREE02 –> download template--> ‘Create or update accrual objects with the total amount.

Template Contents:

o Accrual Object Category

o Company code

o Total accrual/deferral amount in transaction currency

o Accrual Method 

o Start of life and end of life

o Accrual item type

o Ledger group

o Transaction currency

· How It Works: The system uses the total amount and the specified accrual method to automatically calculate and distribute the amounts over the relevant periods.

2. Upload of Pre-calculated Accrual Amounts with Creation of Accrual Objects

 Purpose: To upload pre-calculated accrual amounts for specific periods. If the accrual object does not already exist, it will be created automatically.

· Template Contents:

o Company Code

o Accrual Object Category

o Last Day of Period

o Start of Life

o End of Life

o Accrual Item Type

o Ledger Group

o Periodic Accrual Amount in transaction Currency

o Transaction Currency

How It Works:

o If the accrual object does not exist, the system will create it.

o If the accrual object exists, the system will update it by adding the uploaded amounts to the existing amounts.

3. Upload of Pre-calculated Accrual Amounts for Some Periods

· Purpose: To upload pre-calculated accrual amounts for specific periods of already existing accrual objects.

· Template Contents:

o Company Code

o Accrual Object Category

o Accrual Object Number

o Last Day of Period

o Accrual Item Type

o Ledger Group

o Periodic Accr. Amount in Transac. Crcy

o Transaction Currency

· How It Works:

o The system updates the existing accrual object with the uploaded amounts for the specified periods.

o The accrual amounts are assumed to have been calculated outside the system and are directly uploaded to the accrual engine for posting.

Summary

· Template 1: Total amount with accrual distribution method.

· Template 2: Pre-calculated amounts for all periods, with object creation if necessary.

· Template 3: Pre-calculated amounts for specific periods of existing accrual objects.

Using these templates effectively allows you to manage accruals flexibly, whether you need to define the distribution method, upload pre-calculated amounts, or update existing accrual objects with specific period amounts.

The excel template can be downloaded from ACACTREE01/02 transaction and can be used for different use cases

entry.title: Re: Even after reversing the payment, it still appears in...
entry.content:
Dear @Devi_Narasimha1,

In Public Cloud this behavior is a known BCM/payment approval scenario. When the payment document is reversed, the related payment batch may still remain visible in the approval process with status In Approval. Therefore the Approve Bank Payments queue alone should not be used as the final indicator that the underlying payment is still valid for approval. SAP documents this exact case in KBA 3512429 for Public Cloud

The approver should identify such cases by checking the payment batch in Display Status of Payment Documents app F7084. This app is the relevant standard control point because it shows the payment document status together with the BCM/payment batch status

More details below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003512429

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F7084')/S36

Recommended review procedure:

  1. In Approve Bank Payments, take the Payment Batch Number from the approval item, for example batch 473 in the screenshot
  2. Open Display Status of Payment Documents (F7084)
  3. Search/check the same payment batch or payment run
  4. If the payment document status is Reversed / Payment Reversed, while the BCM batch status is still In Approval the batch contains a cancelled payment that will not result in an actual outgoing payment, regardless of approval outcome
  5. Per the KBA, the approver can approve the batch the reversed payment documents will be ignored and not included in the payment medium. If all payment documents in the batch are reversed the batch will automatically move to status Automatically Rejected. The reopened invoice items will be re-picked by a subsequent payment run

The Approve Bank Payments app is still the correct approval app and SAP describes it as the app used to review/process payment batches and approve, reject, or defer payments/batches. However for this reversed-payment scenario the additional status check must be done through F7084 because the approval queue may still show the batch even after the payment document has been reversed

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F0673A')/S36

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003460439

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Detailed use cases for Excel upload - Uploading Pre-calculated Accrual Amounts for Each Period
entry.content:
Uploading Pre-calculated Accrual Amounts for Each Period

Pre-requisites:

 Existing Accrual Objects: Ensure that the accrual objects into which you plan to upload the amounts already exist in the system.

EXCEL Template: You need to download the specific EXCEL template designed for this purpose.

Steps:

1. Download the EXCEL Template:

o Navigate to the transaction ACACTREE01/02.

o In this transaction, you will find the option to download the corresponding EXCEL template. Refer to the provided screenshot for assistance.

2. Prepare the EXCEL Template:

o Open the downloaded template.

o Populate the template with the specific accrual amounts for each period for the existing accrual objects. Make sure to align the data correctly with the relevant columns.

3. Upload the EXCEL Template:

o Once the template is populated with the correct data, save it.

o Go back to transaction ACACTREE01/02 (or the appropriate upload interface in the system).

o Use the upload function to upload the populated EXCEL template.

What Happens During the Upload:

· The system will read the uploaded EXCEL file and match the accrual amounts with the existing accrual objects based on the accrual object IDs provided in the template.

· The pre-calculated amounts will be directly uploaded and added to the respective accrual objects for the specified periods.

Benefits of This Approach:

  • Precision: You have complete control over the amounts for each period, allowing for precise and accurate uploads.
  • Flexibility: This method is ideal for situations where the amounts for each period are derived from complex calculations or external systems, which are better handled outside the S/4HANA environment.
  • Efficiency: By directly uploading pre-calculated amounts, you bypass the need for the system to calculate distributions, which can save processing time.

Important Notes:

  • Ensure the integrity and accuracy of the data in the EXCEL template before uploading.
  • Confirm that the accrual objects you are uploading are already set up in the system.
  • Regularly backup your data before performing bulk uploads to safeguard against potential errors.
  • By following these steps and guidelines, you can effectively manage the upload of pre-calculated accrual amounts in the S/4HANA accrual engine.

To enable the upload of pre-calculated accrual amounts for each period in the S/4HANA Accrual Engine, you need to set specific indicators in the customizing settings. Here are the detailed steps and prerequisites:

Prerequisites:

1. Customizing Settings for Accrual Item Types:

o Navigate to the SAP IMG (Implementation Guide) menu.

o Go to the following path: Accruals Management -> Basic Settings -> Accrual Item Types -> Define Item Types.

2. Set the Indicator "Upload Allowed":

o For the relevant accrual item types, set the indicator "Upload Allowed". This permits the system to accept uploaded data for these item types.

3. Uncheck the Indicator "No zero amount":

o In the same customizing activity, uncheck the indicator "No zero amount". This is necessary because you are uploading amounts for individual periods rather than entering a total amount. In this scenario, the total amount is effectively zero since you are specifying period-level amounts directly.

entry.title: Re: How to make story NOT planning enabled by default
entry.content:
@timsmyth 

I do not think there is a way to auto disable planning in stories. Its a toggle available but can be changed. entry.title: Calling SAP CAP API protected by SAP IAS from ABAP
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a SAP CAP app that uses IAS for authentication and authorization. The app is deployed on BTP and provides some UI5 apps via Workzone. This all works fine. The users have AMS capabilities assigned and can access the apps and call corresponding backend services. 

The app provides APIs that will be called from ABAP AS. ABAP will have RFC destination that points to API endpoints and will use oAuth2 for authentication. The destination itself will be consumed by ABAP programs. I would like to have two technical users that will have different AMS capabilities assigned. Here I am struggling how to define these users. I tried the following approaches.

1. In IAS, I generated a cert in section Client Authentication / Certificates of my app. I used it to setup oAuth2 profile in ABAP system. I am able to get an access token and authentication works fine. The problem is with authorization. API logs the following error "403 - Error: User 'system' is lacking required roles: ". The problem is clear to me but I have no idea how to assign AMS capabilities to this technical user.

2. I created a technical user in SAP IAS and assigned AMS capabilties to this user. The problem here is I have no idea how to use this user in oAuth2 profile in ABAP AS. If I have an IAS user, how can I use username/password of this user to call API.

The documentation seems to be really fragmented, having XSUAA and IAS does not help. How can this simple requirement of consuming CAP API from ABAP via technical user can be achieved? Is there a good tutorial for this?

Thanks

entry.title: Building a “Plug-and-Play” Navigation Bar in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
TL; DR: Composite widget in SAC lets us combine widgets, styling, and scripting in a single widget that can be used in multiple stories. This blog walks through the development of a navigational bar composite widget where text, menu hierarchy and icons are fully configurable and centrally controlled. They are controlled either through a function in the composite widget that contains all the parameters required to render the widget or by passing the parameters from the host story using arguments. These arguments are interpreted at runtime and renders the widget accordingly.

 

1.    What is a composite in SAC?

If you have experience in developing few reports/ dashboards, you have likely repeated the same UI element or functions in multiple stories. Eg, header, navigation menu, filter panels, custom menus, footers, page initialization functions, etc.

Composites are meant to solve this problem by letting us build reusable elements of story and maintain them centrally. This helps reduce development and maintenance effort and makes standardization easier.

While the theory makes sense, how many times have we had to tinker with the font size, layout, adjust text boxes, button, etc. after copy pasting a perfectly designed widget into a new story because the requirements were slightly different.

 

2.    How can we make composites even more reusable?

We already have many ways to change the composite widget behaviour through functionalities like interface properties and passing arguments through functions.

Interface Properties and Functions

But can we push even further before crossing into the “custom widget” territory?

 

3.    A Centrally Controlled Navigation Bar

3.1  Goal

Our goal here is to develop a flexible navigational bar that can be reused across stories. It should be easily configured by either by-

  1. Duplicating the composite and changing its configuration like text, icons, menu hierarchy from a single place (like a function)
  2. Or passing the configuration through an argument from the host story

Then at runtime, composite should use the menu configuration and render itself accordingly.

3.2  Composite widget features

  • Enable seamless navigation by opening stories directly from the menu bar
  • Support expanding and collapsing the navigation bar to switch between full menu text and icon only views
  • Centralized configuration of menu labels, icons, and menu hierarchy
  • Ability to expand and collapse child menu items from their parent menu
  • Automatically expand the relevant parent menu and highlight the currently active story
  • Allow menu styling and visual behavior to be controlled via CSS

 Collapsed NavigationExpanded NavigationParameter Script Object

 

3.3  Composite widget design

3.3.1    UI structure

 

             

 

 

3.3.2    Define and initialize script variables

Initialize arrays for buttons, texts, and panels into script variables of the composite widget. Since elements are referenced by their index in various functions, we need to ensure that the order of the objects inside the arrays matches UI and “Outline”. Note that the order in the “Outline” is in descending.

 

Maintain the “Selection” type array that will store the various menu properties and its structure, in this case it is “sv_buttonConfig.”

 

3.3.3    Functions and Script Objects

Write script objects and functions for rendering the widget at run time and for various actions like parent button toggle, opening a story on menu button click, highlighting the current story button, etc.

 

 

3.3.4    Control button styling using CSS

 

 

 

4.    What Next?

Some of the functionalities that can be added:

  1. Multi-level menu hierarchy
  2. Theme switching
  3. Role based dynamic menu
  4. Passing current story filters to a new story

 

5.    Conclusion

Composite widgets are quite interesting and opens up new avenues to explore standardization. This blog is just a simple example of how far one can go with changing composite widget behaviour from one story to another. Instead of looking at composite widget as repeatable object, we can start viewing it as configurable building block.

entry.title: Top 9 takeaways #SAPSapphire 2026
entry.content:
1. The ERP Brain Has a New Job. It Works for Your Agents Now.
SAP Business AI Platform
 
1. SAP Business AI Platform

 

Almost every company is using AI. Almost none are getting the business value they expected. The Stanford AI survey Christian Klein referenced at the open says what many CIOs already know privately: the problem isn't the model. The problem is everything below the waterline: 7 million data fields, thousands of proprietary processes, authorization rules, governance requirements. None of which any model was trained on, and none of which any model naturally respects.

SAP's answer is the SAP Business AI Platform, launched at Sapphire 2026. Its core is a rich context layer that injects ERP domain knowledge directly into every agent interaction. Knowledge graphs navigate thousands of business processes and select the right data. SAP Domain Models give agents native understanding of business logic. Business Data Cloud provides a single semantic layer across SAP and non-SAP systems. The governance layer checks identity and authorization before any output surfaces.

JPMorgan Chase joined the keynote to make this concrete. The bank processes 12 trillion dollars in payments across 120 currencies every day and is already deploying agentic solutions that catch errors before they post to the ledger. Jeremy Barnum, CFO, JPMorgan Chase:
"AI is only as good as the data and processes underneath it. It can't reach its full potential in a fragmented legacy environment."
The framing is deliberate. SAP is not adding AI to ERP. It is bringing 50 years of ERP domain knowledge into AI. That is a different claim, and a structural one.


2. 224 Agents, 51 Assistants. The Pilot Era is Over.
The Autonomous Suite
 
2. The Autonomous Suite

 

Enterprise AI has lived in pilot purgatory for two years. Impressive demos, limited deployment, unclear ROI. The structural problem is that most agentic platforms are horizontal: they give you a foundation and then require you to build your own business process agents from scratch. SAP is taking the opposite position.

The Autonomous Suite launches with 224 pre-built agents and 51 assistants across five domains: Autonomous Finance, Autonomous Spend, Autonomous Supply Chain, Autonomous HCM, and Autonomous CX. These are outcome-driven, SOX audit-compatible, ISO-certified agents mapped to specific business roles with defined KPIs. Each is extensible: you can add tools, workflow steps, or custom agents for what does not fit out of the box.

Early customers are already in production. Bayer is using the Cash Collections Assistant to accelerate cash flow. Ericsson deployed intelligent role recommendations across 85,000 employees. Petco is using the Merchandising Assistant to surface assortment gaps and optimize product data across all channels. The number of agents and assistants will grow monthly.


3. Company Memory: SAP Decides to Solve the Tribal Knowledge Problem
Company Memory
 
3. Company Memory

 

Every company has two kinds of knowledge. The kind in the system: structured, documented, queryable. And the kind in the heads of the people who have run the process for 15 years and are no longer in the building. The second kind is worth more. It disappears first.

SAP introduced Company Memory as a knowledge management layer for the Autonomous Suite. Built on the Signavio foundation, it ingests process models, policy documents, Teams and Slack channels, and email approval chains. From that unstructured material, it extracts "process atoms": small structured units of business knowledge that tell agents what to look for, what to do, and what not to do. Every override, every pattern, every correction becomes instruction for the next run.

You can directly update any process atom manually, and every agent in your landscape adapts instantly. The system is not learning autonomously without oversight. It is learning with you. The Financial Closing demo illustrated this: 12 legal entities, four ERP systems, six currencies, hundreds of reconciliations. Q2's close was measurably faster than Q1. Each close teaches the next one.


4. Joule Gets a Ground-Up Rebuild. The Internal Name Was V10.
Joule Work
4. Joule Work

 

SAP internally called this the V10 of Joule. The marketing team called it Joule Work instead. The distinction between the current Joule and Joule Work is structural, not incremental.

The current Joule is RAG-based, limited in context and governance. Joule Work introduces a full agentic harness with computer and file access, open standards support including MCP, and a more complete knowledge base. The new Joule Spaces interface creates contextual workspaces that generate dynamically based on the task at hand. SAP's framing is "App-less": an experience layer where users interact with their underlying applications without having to be in them.

The Financial Closing demo gave Joule Spaces its clearest illustration. The Closing Assistant surfaced 18 items requiring human review inside a single workspace: accrual proposals with confidence levels, intercompany variances already resolved, one-click reconciliation across currencies and entities. The human stayed in the loop without ever opening a separate application. That is what "appless" looks like in practice.


5. Build Agents for Outcomes, Not Architecture: Joule Studio 2.0
Joule Studio 2.0
 
5. Joule Studio 2.0

 

The standard criticism of agentic AI tools is that they ask the builder to think like an engineer when the business needs them to think like a process owner. Joule Studio 2.0 is designed to invert that sequence.

The new Studio is intent-based: you describe the business challenge, not the technical specification. Philip Herzig demonstrated this with a pharma distribution scenario: margin leakage of $24 million identified via SAP Signavio. Joule Studio took that diagnosis, generated a full product requirements document, identified two existing agents already covering part of the workflow, and built the full orchestration without writing a single line of code. KPMG is targeting $120 million in reduced contract leakage using this stack.

Joule Studio 2.0 is model-agnostic: bring any third-party LLM or SAP's own. Development is low-code, pro-code, or anything in between. The evaluation framework is data-driven, so you validate agent behavior against actual business outcomes. It is available free through end of year.


6. Industry AI: The Superpower SAP Has Been Sitting On
Industry AI
 
6. Industry AI

 

Sebastian Steinhauser opened with a raised hand. Who is using SAP 100% in standard? Almost none. Who is using SAP with industry-specific solutions? Most of the room. That gap has historically been filled by customizations and the expertise of people who have been in the industry for 20 years.

Industry AI is SAP's move to build that depth in. Seven autonomous industry solutions are launching now, spanning adaptive production, project delivery, regulated manufacturing, revenue growth management, and unified commerce. Take regulated manufacturing: in pharma, every batch must be traced and audit-ready. The Industry AI solution delivers multi-agent processes that detect disruptions early and simulate compliant recovery options in real time. Takeda reports up to 25% reduction in revenue loss from stockouts.

H&M offered the fullest picture. 4,100 stores across 81 markets, with a Store Intelligence Agent coordinating end-to-end across the value chain in real time. Ellen Swanstrom, Chief Digital and Information Officer, H&M:
"To unlock the full potential of new technologies like AI, the right digital foundation is needed to ensure that we can standardize and harmonize key business processes. And SAP sits at the core of our processes."


7. Below the Waterline: Dremio, Prior Labs, and SAP-RPT-1.5
Business Data Cloud
 
7. Business Data Cloud

 

Three data announcements in fifteen minutes. Any one of them would have been the headline at a smaller event.

SAP intends to acquire Dremio, a high-performance data lakehouse with federation across any cloud and legacy database. Combined with Business Data Cloud, this enables agent reasoning across any cloud environment without data movement. SAP also intends to acquire Prior Labs, whose TabPFN model family handles non-SAP structured data for prediction use cases including financial trading. And SAP is launching SAP-RPT-1.5, the next generation of its own tabular foundation model, with a chat API for what-if analysis, predictive simulation, and built-in explainability, without any model training required.

Together, SAP-RPT-1.5 and TabPFN address the part of enterprise AI that large language models were never designed for: accurate, explainable, prediction-grade outcomes on tabular business data. The LLM is the tip of the iceberg. The data layer being assembled here is everything underneath it.


8. One Command Center for Every Agent You Didn't Build: AI Agent Hub
AI Agent Hub
 
8. AI Agent Hub

 

By now, most large enterprises have more AI agents running than anyone has officially counted. Some are great. Some are not. Almost no one has a consistent picture of which agents are operating, what they are allowed to do, and whether they comply with governance requirements.

SAP AI Agent Hub, built on LeanIX (already used by more than 25% of the Fortune 500), is the central command center for all of it. Teams can discover, manage, and govern SAP agents, non-SAP agents, and MCP servers across the landscape. Telemetry routes to Cloud ALM for health monitoring. Signavio's agent mining surfaces optimization opportunities against KPIs. All agent activity maps to SuccessFactors so the workforce impact is visible end-to-end.

Three things worth noting: governing non-SAP agents is free. A2A interoperability with third-party agentic platforms is also free. And the full governance lifecycle is included in SAP Business AI Platform at no extra charge. The governance infrastructure is being given away. The value proposition is the agents running on top of it.


9. Rise and Grow with SAP: Change Management Written Into the Deal
Rise and Grow with SAP
 
9. Rise and Grow with SAP

 


The keynote offered a clear framing: "Technology alone and plugging AI agents into your existing system landscape will drive zero value." That framing is deliberate. Rise and Grow with SAP is built to answer it directly.

The new Rise and Grow with SAP offering responds directly. SAP commits to sending architects, AI developers, and industry consultants on-site to drive change management and AI adoption, regardless of whether the customer starts on S/4HANA or ECC. The contractual commitment: activate three dual assistants of your choice within year one. For migration, Joule for developers and Joule for consultants are being extended with AI assistants targeting a 50% reduction in total migration effort.

The tooling behind that commitment is now visible. Seven specialized migration assistants, covering system analysis, data management, custom code, configuration, test management, rollout, and project management, are orchestrated inside Cloud ALM. Joule for Consultants is the single entry point across all of them. System analysis that previously required weeks of preparation can now run over a weekend.

Technology. Change management. Migration. These are the three failure modes of large-scale ERP transformation, and SAP is now naming all three in the same commercial offer. The argument is not that SAP's AI is the best AI. It is that no matter where you start, SAP will make you ready to use AI tomorrow.

entry.title: Re: Custom field not coming in PO print xml structure
entry.content:
Dear @Sudharmv,

This behavior is standard for Purchase Order Limit / Enhanced Limit items

The custom field is available for standard PO item categories because these items use the standard Purchase Order Item node, which supports custom field extension and mapping to the PO output form. For Enhanced Limit items the output XML uses the separate PurchaseOrderLimitItemNode. SAP confirms that this structure is not extensible through Custom Fields and Logic and therefore custom fields cannot be added or displayed in the PO form output for Limit items

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3347527

This means the issue is not caused by OPD the Adobe form layout the custom form template assignment or missing enablement of the custom field for PO APIs/forms. If the custom field is not present in the generated XML for the Enhanced Limit item the form cannot print it

The practical options are:

  1. Use a standard field/text that is already available in the Limit Item XML node, if it can cover the business requirement
  2. If a dedicated custom field is required specifically for Enhanced Limit item output, raise a Customer Influence request as SAP currently treats this as a product enhancement request

More details below:

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#

BestRegards,

Dawid

entry.title: Stay Informed When Product Cost Posting Errors Occur
entry.content:
Introduction and Background

For event-based production cost postings, WIP (work-in-progress) and variance document postings are linked to and triggered by logistic business transactions (also known as business events include goods issue, activity confirmation, goods receipt, technical completion of order). These posting mechanisms automate production costing postings, simplify and accelerate period end closing processes. This also means postings occur in the background, and when errors do occasionally occur, the above-mentioned logistics transactions will not be interrupted or show any errors about failures of WIP and variance postings. Users will need to head over to Manage Event-Based Posting Errors - Product Costing to view errors or Postprocess Event-Based Postings - Product Costing to view and reprocess postings.

Errors most often occur when scope item 3F0 have recently been implemented when configurations and G/L account master data issue account for the root causes of most error messages. After the SAP system has gone live for a while, the frequency of errors will drop, and users may gradually get accustomed to not viewing errors in these monitor apps. This situation may result in errors going undetected.

In this blog, I would like to introduce two ways to monitor errors without first opening any Fiori apps.  1st approach is using insight cards, the 2nd approach is using situation handling, which requires slightly more effort to set up but has more features and is more collaborative than using insight cards.

Insight card

First, let’s start with Order Exception Monitor - Event-Based. This is a dashboard app that shows the event-based production cost posting errors that need to be resolved and the unbalanced orders to be posted, and it has several predefined cards.

As depicted in the screenshot below, click Add Card to Insights.

Provide a card name and subtitle.

Open My Home Settings on Fiori launchpad homepage, navigate to Insight Cards, make visible of previously saved card.

Check insight card section on the homepage, the card that contains the error list from Order Exception Monitor - Event-Based. Clicking of the errors will get navigated to Manage Product Costing Errors to simulate posting or Postprocess Event-Based Postings - Product Costing to perform posting.

Situation Handling

Since CE2602 release, a situation template Event-Based Product Costing Errors (FIN_EBPC_ERROR_MONITOR) has been introduced. A custom situation type can be created using the Fiori app Manage Situation Types – Extended and to receive notifications when event-based order posting errors occur. Let's see how it works in below steps.

Create a situation type

Situation Triggers indicates when errors are created, a situation will be generated.

Recipients refer to the teams containing the users who receive the notification.

In Manage Teams and Responsibilities, maintain the team to be used in situation type. The teams may include production accountants, IT users etc.

After the situation type is created and enabled. The assigned users will receive notifications and situations on homepage

Clicking a situation will show detailed error messages and affected orders. Alongside features in situation handling framework, a situation can be assigned and closed.

In the Event-Based order section, several apps can be used to view and handle errors. The most frequently used  is Postprocess Event-Based Postings - Product Costing which performs posting for the affected orders once the root causes  have been identified and fixed.

Summary

The blog introduced two approaches to monitor errors. 1st approach is using insight cards, which is very easy to use and may be suitable for end users for tracking errors alongside some important KPIs and other insight cards ; 2nd approach is using situation handling framework. This approach needs some steps to set up, but is more powerful, as it can trigger notifications to the right users based on teams set-up, and the situation case has a status management.

You are encouraged to give a try and provide us with some feedback.  Speaking of error handling, there is also a great AI feature to proactively identify and resolve errors in production cost postings, refer to  AI-Assisted Error Resolution for Cost Accounting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

entry.title: Re: Send Partner Function of BP to Hybris via DRFOUT Transaction
entry.content: When a customer is replicated from S4 via CPI, we receive two payloads: one for the B2B unit(BusinessPartnerSUITEBulkReplicateRequest_Out) and another for the B2B customer(BusinessPartnerRelationshipSUITEBulkReplicateRequest_Out ). Currently, CPI uses the default B2B unit reference from the customer payload, as this payload contains all the necessary customer relationships. In contrast, the B2B unit payload does not include any customer-related information. To ensure that the correct default B2B unit is sent to Hybris, we need the required identifier to be present in the B2Bcustomer payload.
